From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

English film director and producer (1888–1958)

**John E. Blakeley** (1 October 1888 – 20 February 1958) was a British [film producer](/wiki/Film_producer "Film producer"), [director](/wiki/Director_(film) "Director (film)") and [screenwriter](/wiki/Screenwriter "Screenwriter"), the founder of [Mancunian Films](/wiki/Mancunian_Films "Mancunian Films").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/f/fc/Film_producer_John_E._Blakeley.jpg)](/wiki/File:Film_producer_John_E._Blakeley.jpg)

John E. Blakeley

Born in [Ardwick](/wiki/Ardwick "Ardwick"), [Manchester](/wiki/Manchester "Manchester"), son of James Blakeley (born c. 1862; Manchester), and Margaret Quirk (born c. 1861; Glasgow, Scotland), he was of Lancashire (Church of England) descent on his father's side of the family and [Irish Catholic](/wiki/Irish_Catholic "Irish Catholic") on his mother's. His father had become an early [film distributor](/wiki/Film_distributor "Film distributor") in 1908 after previous work as a travelling draper. Blakeley joined his father's business and soon came to understand the tastes of the emerging [cinema](/wiki/Film "Film") audiences in the northern industrial towns. By the 1930s, the younger Blakeley was making films starring the idols of northern [music hall](/wiki/Music_hall "Music hall") [comedy](/wiki/Comedy "Comedy"): [George Formby](/wiki/George_Formby,_Jr. "George Formby, Jr."), [Frank Randle](/wiki/Frank_Randle "Frank Randle")[[1]](#cite_note-1) and [Sandy Powell](/wiki/Sandy_Powell_(comedian) "Sandy Powell (comedian)").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/59/Dickenson_Road_Studios.jpg/250px-Dickenson_Road_Studios.jpg)](/wiki/File:Dickenson_Road_Studios.jpg)

[Dickenson Road Studios](/wiki/Dickenson_Road_Studios "Dickenson Road Studios") in Manchester during their BBC period, c. 1965

Initially relying on studios in London, rising costs encouraged him to found the Mancunian Film Studios in his hometown in 1947. With £70,000 [capital](/wiki/Financial_capital "Financial capital"), a former Methodist chapel on Dickenson Road in [Rusholme](/wiki/Rusholme "Rusholme") was converted into a film studio.[[2]](#cite_note-2) The studios produced a sequence of profitable films, often on shoestring budgets, until Blakeley's retirement in 1953. The following year the studios were sold to the BBC.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Blakeley died in [Stockport](/wiki/Stockport "Stockport") aged 69.

## Filmography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_E._Blakeley&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Filmography")]

### Director

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_E._Blakeley&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Director")]

* *[Dodging the Dole](/wiki/Dodging_the_Dole "Dodging the Dole")* (1936)
* *[Somewhere in England](/wiki/Somewhere_in_England_(film) "Somewhere in England (film)")* (1940)
* *[Somewhere in Camp](/wiki/Somewhere_in_Camp "Somewhere in Camp")* (1942)
* *[Somewhere on Leave](/wiki/Somewhere_on_Leave "Somewhere on Leave")* (1942)
* *[Home Sweet Home](/wiki/Home_Sweet_Home_(1945_film) "Home Sweet Home (1945 film)")* (1945)
* *[Demobbed](/wiki/Demobbed_(1946_film) "Demobbed (1946 film)")* (1946)
* *[Under New Management](/wiki/Under_New_Management "Under New Management")* (1946)
* *[Holidays with Pay](/wiki/Holidays_with_Pay "Holidays with Pay")* (1948)
* *[Somewhere in Politics](/wiki/Somewhere_in_Politics "Somewhere in Politics")* (1948)
* *[Cup-Tie Honeymoon](/wiki/Cup-Tie_Honeymoon "Cup-Tie Honeymoon")* (1948)
* *[What a Carry On!](/w/index.php?title=What_a_Carry_On!&action=edit&redlink=1 "What a Carry On! (page does not exist)")* (1949)
* *[School for Randle](/wiki/School_for_Randle "School for Randle")* (1949)
* *[Over the Garden Wall](/wiki/Over_the_Garden_Wall_(1950_film) "Over the Garden Wall (1950 film)")* (1950)
* *[Let's Have a Murder](/wiki/Let%27s_Have_a_Murder "Let's Have a Murder")* (1950)
* *[It's a Grand Life](/wiki/It%27s_a_Grand_Life "It's a Grand Life")* (1953)

### Producer

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_E._Blakeley&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Producer")]

* *[The Penny Pool](/wiki/The_Penny_Pool "The Penny Pool")* (1937)
* *[Calling All Crooks](/wiki/Calling_All_Crooks "Calling All Crooks")* (1938)
